---
title: "DATA 402 HW 4"
author: "Sophia Chung"
format: 
  html:
    embed-resources: true
execute:
  echo: true
---

#### Section A: Derivations

![](/Users/sophiapchung/Desktop/DATA%20402/HW4/A.jpeg)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

#### Section B: Coding

##### 1)

```{r}
cannabis <- read.csv('/Users/sophiapchung/Desktop/DATA 402/HW4/cannabis_full.csv')
cannabis <- cannabis[complete.cases(cannabis[,6:69]),]
```

```{r}
fit_lasso <- function(X, Y, lambda, eta) {
  n <- nrow(X)
  p <- ncol(X)
  betas <- rep(0, p)
  
  # helper functions
  stopping_condition <- function(curr_steps, max_steps=1000, eta, cutoff=1e-9) {
    curr_steps >= max_steps || eta < cutoff
  }
  
  compute_gradient <- function(X, Y, betas, lambda) {
    gradient <- -2/n * (t(X) %*% (Y - X %*% betas)) + lambda * sign(betas)
    return (gradient)
  }
  
  curr_steps <- 0
  while (!stopping_condition(curr_steps, eta=eta)) {
    gradient <- compute_gradient(X, Y, betas, lambda)
    betas <- betas - eta*gradient
    curr_steps <- curr_steps + 1
  }
  return (betas)
}
```

##### 2)

```{r}
tune_lambda_cv_lasso <- function(X, Y, metric, lambdas, splits) {
  indices <- sample(1:nrow(X))
  folds <- split(indices, cut(indices, breaks=splits, labels=FALSE))
  
  avg_metrics <- c()
  for (i in 1:length(lambdas)) {
    metrics <- c()
    for (j in 1:splits) {
      test_indices <- folds[[j]]
      
      X_test <- X[folds[[j]],]
      y_test <- Y[folds[[j]],]
      X_train <- X[-folds[[j]],]
      y_train <- Y[-folds[[j]],]
      
      # eta that works for this data set
      betas <- fit_lasso(X_train, y_train, lambdas[i], 0.00001)
      pred <- X_test %*% betas
      
      if (metric == 'R-squared') {
        r_squared <- 1 - sum((y_test - pred)^2)/sum((y_test - mean(y_test))^2)
        metrics[j] <- r_squared
      }
      else if (metric == 'MSE') {
        metrics[j] <- mean(y_test - pred^2)
      }
      else if (metric == 'MAE') {
        metrics[j] <- mean(abs(y_test - pred))
      }
    }
    avg_metrics[i] <- mean(metrics)
  }
  
  if (metric == 'R-squared') {
    best_lambda <- lambdas[which.max(avg_metrics)]
  }
  else {
    best_lambda <- lambdas[which.min(avg_metrics)]
  }
  df <- data.frame('lambda'=lambdas, 'metric'=avg_metrics, 'best_lambda'=best_lambda)
  return (df)
}
```

##### 3)

```{r}
X <- data.matrix(subset(cannabis, select=-c(Strain, Rating, Effects, Flavor)))
X <- cbind(rep(1, nrow(cannabis)), X)
Y <- data.matrix(subset(cannabis, select=c(Rating)))
head(tune_lambda_cv_lasso(X, Y, 'R-squared', seq(1, 100, by=1), 3))
```

Using R-squared, the best $\lambda$ is 1.

##### 4)

```{r}
fit_lasso(Y, X, 1, 0.00001)
```

The intercept or $\beta_0$ is the largest $\beta$ value in this model. We can interpret it: if a cannabis strain is of the baseline Type (whichever Type R chose as default) and none of the keywords are used to describe its effect, then its predicted rating is 0.071.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

#### Section C: Concepts

##### 1)

* Method 1: Set each $\beta_j$ to its average predicted value regardless of other predictors. These values could very well be closer to the actual values than to zero.
* Method 2: Set each $\beta_j$ to 1, to see how the model would perform if all predictors contributed equally.
* Method 3: Set each $\beta_j$ to $1/j$, to see how the model would perform if all predictors contributed equally, but at a smaller scale.

##### 2)

```{r}
gd_ridge <- function(x, y, lambda, eta) {
  n <- length(x)
  betas <- rep(0, 2)
  
  # helper functions
  stopping_condition <- function(curr_steps, max_steps=1000, eta, cutoff=1e-9) {
    curr_steps >= max_steps || eta < cutoff
  }
  
  compute_gradient <- function(x, y, betas, lambda) {
    gradient0 <- -2/n * sum(y - rep(betas[1], n)) + 2*lambda*betas[1]
    gradient1 <- -2/n * sum((y - rep(betas[2], n))*x) + 2*lambda*betas[2]
    gradient <- c(gradient0, gradient1)
    return (gradient)
  }
  
  curr_steps <- 0
  while (!stopping_condition(curr_steps, eta=eta) && curr_steps < 2) {
    cat('Update', curr_steps + 1, '\n')
    gradient <- compute_gradient(x, y, betas, lambda)
    cat('Gradient:', gradient, '\n')
    betas <- betas - eta*gradient
    cat('New betas:', betas, '\n')
    curr_steps <- curr_steps + 1
  }
}
```

##### a)

```{r}
sqft <- c(1500, 2200, 3700, 4100)
price <- c(1, 2, 3, 14)
gd_ridge(sqft, price, lambda=10, eta=0.01)
```

##### b)

```{r}
gd_ridge(sqft, price, lambda=10, eta=1)
```
